title: rhetinocarpha suffruticosa
intro: Rhetinocarpha suffruticosa, also known as the "Karee bush," is a shrub-like plant that belongs to the family Asteraceae. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial compounds. The health benefits of Rhetinocarpha suffruticosa are said to include reducing fever, relieving pain and inflammation, and treating various infections. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness.
